subject: [PATCH] mm: vmscan: fix dirty folios throttling on cgroup v1 for MGLRU

reviews.author: Kairui Song
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Kairui Song noted that Baolin's fix is great but suggested reworking the throttling mechanism for MGLRU and mentioned they have already posted a separate series addressing this issue.

Reviewer Kairui Song questioned the relevance of the patch for cgroup v1, noting that it only addresses this version and suggesting that the focus should be on improving the page counter as per Jingxiang's plan.
reviews.raw_body:
Right, I think Baolin's fix is great, but to improve the whole trottling
mechanism we need some rework for MGLRU, currently I posted another
series for this.

---

For example memsw?

https://lore.kernel.org/linux-mm/q2x4drxpjbxcxgns6bjp446ynsxgl32ckcljqcol7posds4nit@3n3tjq35anvb/

I remember Jingxiang's plan is to improve the page counter first.

reviews.author: Baolin Wang
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Baolin Wang noted that Kairui's patch still needs to unify the throttling in shrink_node(), citing previous discussions by Shakeel and Johannes, but acknowledged significant improvements over the previous version and no observed premature OOM issues.

Reviewer noted that there are existing users of cgroup v1 who will require a migration period before switching to cgroup v2, and requested consideration for this timeline.
reviews.raw_body:
Agree. As both Shakeel and Johannes mentioned, I think we still need to 
unify the throttling in shrink_node(). That said, Kairui\u2019s series is 
much improved over before and I haven\u2019t observed any premature OOM 
issues so far from my testing.

---

Additionally, we still have some existing users on cgroup v1, and they 
will need some time to migrate to cgroup v2.

2026-04-02.patch_summary: This patch fixes an issue where dirty folios are throttled too aggressively in cgroup v1 for MGLRU (Mostly Gone, Least Recently Used). The problem arises from the fact that the throttling check happens after a full scan of the given memory cgroup's LRU vector, leading to more aggressive throttling than traditional LRU. The patch aims to address this issue by adjusting the throttling behavior.

reviews.author: Shakeel Butt (author)
reviews.summary: Author responded to Baolin Wang's concern about the patch being v1 only, explaining that he is working on a memcg_ext proposal to provide similar behavior and plans to have a prototype before LSFMM.
reviews.raw_body:
Time is fine but is there any specific v1 feature which is a blocker for you.
The memsw is a known one and is the same for Google (data center). I am aiming
to provide memsw like behavior through my memcg_ext proposal but performance is
an open question. I am aiming to have a prototype before LSFMM to further the
discussion.
